clinical_indications: Severe Acute Malnutrition (SAM) present along with complications/poor appetite/failed home treatment
6-59 months: MUAC <11.5 cm or WHZ <-3 SD or bilateral pitting oedema

preauth.label: Still image of the child at the time of admission with patient ID and date

Clinical notes with indications (Faulty feeding habits, Poor appetite, Lethargy/Irritability, Any delayed developmental milestones including Weight, Vitals- Pulse rate (PR), respiratory rate (RR), Capillary refill time (CRT), Loss of Subcutaneous fat, muscle wasting, pallor, mid-upper arm circumference (MUAC) less than normal, Signs of Vitamin B, K and A deficiencies (if any of these symptoms are present), Dehydration, Respiratory distress)

Essential Investigations (Haemogram, Random Blood sugar (RBS), LFT, KFT, Chest X-ray, RDT-HIV (only where available/ possible), Gastric aspirate for CBNAAT/ AFB (only where available/ possible), Peripheral smear examination)

claim.label: Still image of the child at the time of discharge with patient ID and date

Detailed indoor case papers with treatment details indicating (Monitoring of vitals with Input-output charting as well as urine frequency, stool/ vomitus volumes, Intake: IV fluids (IVF) (DNS) 4ml/ Kg/hr for 2-3 days with early/ concomitant initiation of oral feeds (130 ml/kg/day), Condition/ complication specific treatment such as Antibiotics for Infection, Dextrose for Hypoglycemia/ severe dehydration, Potassium/ Magnesium for electrolyte imbalance, Whole blood/ PRBC transfusion for Anemia.)

Detailed essential investigation reports (Haemogram, Random Blood sugar (RBS), LFT, KFT, Chest X-ray, RDT-HIV (only where available/ possible), Gastric aspirate for CBNAAT/ AFB (only where available/ possible), Peripheral smear examination)

Detailed Discharge summary with follow-up advise including (Transfer to Nutritional Rehabilitation Centre (NRC), Feeding, electrolytes (Zinc, Copper & Iron), vitamins (A & D, A, B complex) & supplementation advice)

ppd_preauth.q: Is the still image of the child at the time of admission with patient ID and date?

Clinical notes indicating (Faulty feeding habits, Poor appetite, Lethargy/Irritability, Any delayed developmental milestones including Weight, Vitals- Pulse rate (PR), respiratory rate (RR), Capillary refill time (CRT), Loss of Subcutaneous fat, muscle wasting, pallor, mid-upper arm circumference (MUAC) less than normal, Signs of Vitamin A, B and K deficiencies (If any of these symptoms are present), Dehydration, Respiratory distress)?
Essential Investigations (Haemogram, Random Blood sugar (RBS), LFT, KFT, Chest X-ray, RDT-HIV (only where available/ possible), Gastric aspirate for CBNAAT/ AFB (only where available/ possible))?
SAM with Complications/ Poor appetite/ failed home treatment?
Is the Child 0-6 months of age- Yes, then is MUAC< 11.0 cm?
Is the Child 6-59 months of age- Yes, then is MUAC<11.5 cm or WHZ <-3 SD or bilateral pitting edema?
Is the Child > 5years of age- Yes, then is BMI </= 3SD (severe thinness)?

cpd_claim.q: Is the still image of the child at the time of discharge with patient ID and date available?

Does the patient have documentary evidence of (clinical indications & physical examination) for admission for Severe Acute Malnutrition (SAM) such as: Faulty feeding habits (Not exclusively Breast fed for 6 months/ bottle feeding/ delayed or inadequate complementary feeding), Poor appetite, Lethargy/Irritability, Any delayed developmental milestones including Weight, Vitals- Pulse rate (PR), respiratory rate (RR), Capillary refill time (CRT), Loss of Subcutaneous fat, muscle wasting, pallor, mid-upper arm circumference (MUAC) less than normal, Signs of Vitamin A, B, and K deficiencies (If any of these symptoms are present), Dehydration, Respiratory distress?

Do the indoor case papers give the treatment details such as: Monitoring of vitals with Input-output charting as well as urine frequency, stool/ vomitus volumes, Intake: IVF(DNS) 4ml/ Kg/hr for 2-3 days with early/ concomitant initiation of oral feeds (130 ml/kg/day), Condition/ complication specific treatment such as Antibiotics for Infection, Dextrose for Hypoglycemia/ severe dehydration, Potassium/ Magnesium for electrolyte imbalance, Whole blood/ PRBC transfusion for Anemia?

Is there a documentary evidence to confirm the discharge criteria i.e.: Child is clinically well and alert, No or resolving medical complications, No or resolving edema (if present), Satisfactory oral intake, has a good appetite with appropriate weight gain (taking at least 75% of target calorie intake of 150-200 kcal/kg/day & 0-6 months old have weight gain of 3-5 g/kg/day for three days).

Is the discharge summary available and includes follow-up advise for rehabilitation phase such as: Transfer to Nutritional Rehabilitation Centre (NRC), Feeding, electrolytes (Zinc, Copper & Iron), vitamins (A & D, A, B complex), Supplementation advise?

common_queries: SAM not indicated with complications/poor appetite/failed home treatment as per criteria.
MUAC not documented or outside threshold for the child's age group (0-6 months, 6-59 months).
WHZ or bilateral pitting edema not documented or outside threshold for 6-59 months age group.
BMI not documented or outside threshold for children >5 years.
Admission image missing patient ID or date.
Clinical notes missing key indications such as faulty feeding habits, poor appetite, lethargy/irritability, vitals, or MUAC measurement.
Essential investigation reports (e.g., Haemogram, RBS, LFT, KFT, Chest X-ray) missing or incomplete.
Discharge image missing patient ID or date.
Indoor case papers lack details on vital monitoring, fluid/feed intake, or specific treatment for complications.
Discharge criteria not met or not documented (e.g., child not clinically well, complications not resolving, edema present, unsatisfactory oral intake/weight gain).
Discharge summary not available or missing required follow-up advice for rehabilitation.

additional_information.special_empanelment:
1. Availability of appropriately equipped & staffed Neonatal ICU(NICU) / Paediatric ICU (PICU) for admitting children requiring ICU care.
2. Availability of a qualified Paediatrician (as per the above essential qualifications) in the hospital
clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for admission only if Severe Acute Malnutrition (SAM) is present along with Complications/ poor appetite/ failed home treatment
If the patient is not having any complications/ is able to feed well and mother is co-operative, admission may not be required.
Transfer to Nutrition Rehabilitation Centre (NRC) when child meets the discharge criteria & accepts home available foods

Discharge criteria from hospital to outpatient care: i. Child is clinically well and alert ii. No or resolving medical complications iii. No or resolving edema (if present) iv. Satisfactory oral intake, has a good appetite with appropriate weight gain
additional_information.fitness_criteria: Clinically well and alert; no or resolving medical complications; no or resolving oedema (if present), satisfactory oral intake has a good appetite (taking at least 75% of target calorie intake of 150-200 kcal/kg/day & 0-6 months old have weight gain of 3-5 g/kg/day for three days).
additional_information.referral_criteria: Transfer to Nutrition Rehabilitation Centre (NRC) when child meets the discharge criteria & accepts home available foods
